action.reasoning: Observation: Best hidden score is the 80/20 baseline/window ensemble at 0.6043; 70/30 and 85/15 are both slightly worse, suggesting a narrow optimum near 80/20. Analysis: Remaining time allows only a few hidden evaluations because each eval costs ~15 minutes, so prioritize cheap ensemble variants. Rank-normalized blending optimizes ordering more directly than raw-score blending and may improve Spearman even if calibration worsens. Decision: create a rank-normalized 80/20 ensemble, smoke-test predict.py, and submit it.
action.command: python make_ensemble.py --out checkpoints/ens_b80_w20_rank.pkl --w_base 0.8 --w_window 0.2 --rank_normalize && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/ens_b80_w20_rank.pkl --output predictions_ens_b80_w20_rank_smoke.json
